category_title: Students motivated by high scores who restart tasks when challenged
minimal_context: The student is playing the x20 math game on a Nintendo DS to practice single-digit calculations.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's start the x20 game on your Nintendo DS. Go ahead and answer the math problems.

STUDENT: (Looks at the screen and uses his fingers to count) Got it. (Writes the answer with the stylus)

TUTOR: Do the next one.

STUDENT: (Looks away from the screen, appearing bored and distracted) I don't know this one.

TUTOR: Just try to figure it out.

STUDENT: This sum is too hard, I'm not going to get the highest score. (Reaches over and resets the DS console)

options.A: Take the student to another room to calm down away from the Nintendo DS console.
options.B: Respond with enthusiastic praise, expressing pride in his work and encouraging him to keep going.
options.C: Allow the student to work at his own pace and take a break whenever he wants.
options.D: Consistently prompt the student to continue working and complete the math sums on the console.
